questions.question: Which role has the highest level of access in a Notion workspace?

options.explanation: Admins have the highest permissions, including adding/removing members, changing settings, and updating billing.
Members can access all pages shared with the team but cannot manage workspace settings.
Guests are limited to specific pages they are invited to.
Viewer is not a permission level described in the article.

questions.question: According to the guide, which of the following actions is NOT explicitly listed as something an admin can do?

options.explanation: Admins can add and remove members.
Admins have the ability to change workspace settings.
Admins can update billing information.
Deleting a teamspace is not mentioned among the admin capabilities in the article.

questions.question: What type of pages can guests access?

options.text: All pages shared with the team
options.explanation: That level of access belongs to members, not guests.

options.text: Only specific pages that are shared with them
options.explanation: Guests are limited to the individual pages they are invited to.
Public pages are accessible to anyone, not just guests.
Guests do not have access to workspace settings.

questions.question: Which feature lets you share a Notion page with anyone, even if they don’t have a Notion account?

options.explanation: Permission groups control granular page permissions but still require a Notion account.
A read‑only public page can be viewed by anyone without a Notion account.
Guests must be invited and therefore need a Notion account.
Teamspaces organize work for a team but don’t make pages publicly viewable.

questions.question: How do permission groups differ from the standard roles (admin, member, guest)?

options.text: They replace the standard roles entirely
options.explanation: Permission groups supplement, not replace, the existing roles.

options.text: They provide more granular control over specific page permissions
options.explanation: Groups let you fine‑tune who can see or edit particular pages beyond the broad role categories.

options.text: They are used only for billing purposes
options.explanation: Billing is handled by admins; groups are about page access.
Groups add flexibility but do not reduce an admin’s overall capabilities.

questions.question: Where can you browse over 10,000 Notion templates as suggested in the guide?

options.explanation: The guide points readers to the Template gallery for exploring thousands of templates.
Templates are not listed in workspace settings.
The Help Center provides articles, not the full template collection.
YouTube hosts videos, not the template library.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a customizable permission level in the article?

options.explanation: Admin is one of the three permission levels described.
Member is listed as a permission level.
Guest is also a defined permission level.
The article does not mention an "Owner" role.

questions.question: The guide notes a recent sidebar update. What is the primary benefit of this change?

options.text: Improved visual design of the sidebar
options.explanation: Design improvements are not highlighted as the main benefit.

options.text: Makes sharing with the right groups easier
options.explanation: The update is described as helping users share with the appropriate groups more easily.
Performance gains are not mentioned in the article.

options.text: Adds a dark‑mode option to the sidebar
options.explanation: Dark mode is not discussed in relation to the sidebar update.
